name: Summer Blueberry Tart (Tarte Aux Myrtilles D'ete)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Butter, chilled (cut into small pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. For the Pâte Brisée, mix flour and sugar together in a mixing bowl.
2. Add chilled butter cut into small pieces to the flour mixture. Rub with fingers or cut in until crumbly.
3. Whisk egg yolk, lemon juice, and 1 tablespoon water separately.
4. Sprinkle liquid over the dough and continue stirring/folding until a ball forms. Avoid overworking the dough; if needed, add up to one more tablespoon cold water, lightly kneading it into a ball.
5. Roll out the dough on a floured surface into a large circle. Transfer to a 9- or 10-inch pie plate or tart tin. Patch any holes and trim edges. Fold and flute the crust. Chill for 2 hours.
6. Preheat oven to 375°F. Place aluminum foil in the bottom of the crust and fill with dried beans or rice to weigh down during baking. Bake for 15 minutes, then remove foil and beans and bake an additional 5 minutes. Cool.
7. For the Filling, heat blueberries, water, and sugar in a saucepan until berries release juice. Add cornstarch mixture (dissolved cornstarch mixed with cold water) when berries become juicy. Simmer on low heat for about 10 minutes until thickened. Remove from heat and fold in remaining berries.
8. Pour filling into the baked crust, top with remaining berries, and chill for at least one hour before serving plain or topped with fresh whipped cream or sweetened Crème Fraîche if desired.
